868	\begin{lemma}[Tensor-power comparison isomorphism]
869	  \label{lem:sheafTensorPow_add}
870	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorPowAdd}
871	  \uses{def:sheafTensorPow, def:sheafTensorObj}
872	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], "Sheaves of Modules", \S "Invertible modules"
873	  % (Tag 01CU, following `definition-powers') (read from
874	  % references/stacks-modules.tex, L4249-L4254).
875	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
876	  % "With this definition we have canonical isomorphisms
877	  %  $\mathcal{L}^{\otimes n} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X}
878	  %  \mathcal{L}^{\otimes m} \to
879	  %  \mathcal{L}^{\otimes n + m}$, and these isomorphisms
880	  %  satisfy a commutativity and an associativity constraint
881	  %  (formulation omitted)."
882	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], "Sheaves of Modules", Tag 01CU (canonical
883	  power isomorphisms).}
884	  For a line bundle \(\mathcal{L}\) and \(m, m' \in \mathbb{N}\) there is a
885	  canonical isomorphism of sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules
886	  \[
887	    \mu_{m,m'} :
888	    \mathcal{L}^{\otimes m} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{L}^{\otimes m'}
889	    \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\; \mathcal{L}^{\otimes (m+m')},
890	  \]
891	  natural in \(\mathcal{L}\), and these isomorphisms satisfy a commutativity
892	  constraint (\(\mu_{m,m'}\) agrees with \(\mu_{m',m}\) after the braiding) and
893	  an associativity constraint (the two ways of combining
894	  \(\mathcal{L}^{\otimes m} \otimes \mathcal{L}^{\otimes m'} \otimes
895	  \mathcal{L}^{\otimes m''}\) into \(\mathcal{L}^{\otimes (m+m'+m'')}\) agree).
896	\end{lemma}
897	
898	\begin{proof}
899	  \uses{def:sheafTensorPow, def:sheafTensorObj, cor:sheafTensorObjAssoc,
900	    def:tensorObjUnitIso, def:tensorBraiding,
901	    lem:isIso_sheafification_whiskerRight_unit,
902	    lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib,
903	    lem:presheafModule_sheafification_mathlib}
904	  We do \emph{not} build a full monoidal structure on the whole category
905	  \(\ShMod(\mathcal{O}_X)\) --- none is constructed here --- but only assemble
906	  the comparison \(\mu_{m,m'}\) from those structural isomorphisms of
907	  \cref{def:sheafTensorObj} that the induction actually uses: the associator,
908	  the braiding, and the left unitor.
909	
910	  \textbf{The structural maps.} The braiding and the unitors descend to
911	  \(\ShMod(\mathcal{O}_X)\) directly: each is the sheafification of the
912	  corresponding isomorphism of the presheaf symmetric monoidal structure
913	  (\cref{lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib}), and sheafification, being a
914	  functor, carries isomorphisms to isomorphisms. The \emph{associator} is not
915	  the sheafification of a single presheaf isomorphism, because the iterated
916	  sheaf tensor products
917	  \((\mathcal{A} \otimes \mathcal{B}) \otimes \mathcal{C}\) and
918	  \(\mathcal{A} \otimes (\mathcal{B} \otimes \mathcal{C})\) each carry an inner
919	  sheafification (\cref{def:sheafTensorObj}); it is supplied instead by
920	  \cref{cor:sheafTensorObjAssoc}, whose construction clears those inner
921	  sheafifications using the strong-monoidality comparison
922	  \cref{lem:isIso_sheafification_whiskerRight_unit}. These are the only
923	  isomorphisms of sheaves of modules the construction invokes; right-exactness
924	  of the tensor product is never used.
925	
926	  \textbf{Construction of \(\mu_{m,m'}\).} Fix \(m'\) and induct on \(m\). For
927	  \(m = 0\),
928	  \[
929	    \mathcal{L}^{\otimes 0} \otimes \mathcal{L}^{\otimes m'}
930	      = \mathcal{O}_X \otimes \mathcal{L}^{\otimes m'}
931	      \xrightarrow{\ \sim\ } \mathcal{L}^{\otimes m'}
932	  \]
933	  is the left unitor of \cref{def:sheafTensorObj}. For the inductive step the
934	  recursive definition
935	  \(\mathcal{L}^{\otimes (m+1)} = \mathcal{L}^{\otimes m} \otimes \mathcal{L}\)
936	  (\cref{def:sheafTensorPow}) gives
937	  \[
938	    \mathcal{L}^{\otimes (m+1)} \otimes \mathcal{L}^{\otimes m'}
939	    = (\mathcal{L}^{\otimes m} \otimes \mathcal{L}) \otimes
940	      \mathcal{L}^{\otimes m'}
941	    \xrightarrow{\ \alpha\ } \mathcal{L}^{\otimes m} \otimes
942	      (\mathcal{L} \otimes \mathcal{L}^{\otimes m'})
943	    \xrightarrow{\ \mathrm{id} \otimes \beta\ } \mathcal{L}^{\otimes m} \otimes
944	      (\mathcal{L}^{\otimes m'} \otimes \mathcal{L}),
945	  \]
946	  using the associator \(\alpha\) (\cref{cor:sheafTensorObjAssoc}) and then the
947	  braiding \(\beta\) of \cref{def:sheafTensorObj} whiskered into the left
948	  factor. The inverse associator \(\alpha^{-1}\)
949	  (\cref{cor:sheafTensorObjAssoc}) and the inductive comparison \(\mu_{m,m'}\)
950	  whiskered by \(\mathcal{L}\) on the right then carry this to
951	  \[
952	    (\mathcal{L}^{\otimes m} \otimes \mathcal{L}^{\otimes m'}) \otimes
953	      \mathcal{L}
954	    \xrightarrow{\ \mu_{m,m'} \rhd \mathcal{L}\ }
955	    \mathcal{L}^{\otimes (m + m')} \otimes \mathcal{L}
956	    = \mathcal{L}^{\otimes ((m + m') + 1)},
957	  \]
958	  the final equality being the recursive definition. The reindexing
959	  \((m + m') + 1 = (m + 1) + m'\) identifies the target with
960	  \(\mathcal{L}^{\otimes ((m+1) + m')}\), and the composite is
961	  \(\mu_{m+1,m'}\), an isomorphism as a composite of isomorphisms. Naturality in
962	  \(\mathcal{L}\) holds because each constituent comparison is natural.
963	
964	  \textbf{Commutativity and associativity constraints.} The braiding and
965	  unitors are the sheafifications of the symmetric-monoidal coherence data of
966	  \(\PshMod(\mathcal{O}_X)\) (\cref{lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib}), and
967	  the associator is intertwined with the presheaf associator by the comparison
968	  isomorphisms of \cref{lem:isIso_sheafification_whiskerRight_unit} through the
969	  construction of \cref{cor:sheafTensorObjAssoc}. Both the commutativity square
970	  (\(\mu_{m,m'}\) versus \(\mu_{m',m}\) postcomposed with the braiding) and the
971	  associativity pentagon (the two bracketings of
972	  \(\mathcal{L}^{\otimes m} \otimes \mathcal{L}^{\otimes m'} \otimes
973	  \mathcal{L}^{\otimes m''} \to \mathcal{L}^{\otimes (m+m'+m'')}\)) are therefore
974	  images, under the sheafification functor together with these comparison
975	  isomorphisms, of the corresponding coherence diagrams of the presheaf
976	  symmetric monoidal structure, which commute by Mac\,Lane coherence. Since a
977	  functor preserves commuting diagrams and the comparison isomorphisms are
978	  natural, the constraints hold for the family \(\{\mu_{m,m'}\}\). We emphasise
979	  that this argument uses only the single strong-monoidality comparison
980	  \cref{lem:isIso_sheafification_whiskerRight_unit} and the resulting associator
981	  \cref{cor:sheafTensorObjAssoc}, not a full monoidal structure on
982	  \(\ShMod(\mathcal{O}_X)\).
983	\end{proof}

985	\section{Lax-monoidal global sections}
986	\label{sec:sgr_lax_sections}
987	
988	The graded multiplication is supplied by the lax-monoidal structure of the
989	global-sections functor \(\Gamma(X, -) : \ShMod(\mathcal{O}_X) \to
990	\mathrm{ModuleCat}(\Gamma(X, \mathcal{O}_X))\). It is only \emph{lax} because
991	global sections do not commute with sheafification: a global section of a
992	sheafified tensor product is not in general a finite sum of elementary tensors of
993	global sections.
994	
995	\begin{definition}\leanok
996	[Section multiplication]
997	  \label{def:sectionMul}
998	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.sectionsMul}
999	  \uses{def:sheafTensorObj, lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib,
1000	    lem:presheafModule_sheafification_mathlib}
1001	  Let \(\mathcal{F}, \mathcal{G}\) be sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules. The
1002	  \emph{section multiplication} is the \(\Gamma(X,\mathcal{O}_X)\)-bilinear map
1003	  \[
1004	    \Gamma(X, \mathcal{F}) \otimes_{\Gamma(X,\mathcal{O}_X)}
